questions.question: Scope-boundary check: this story's diff must touch only the new `app/tests/e2e/groups-abandon-last-member.spec.ts` file, plus any e2e helper it must extend (per stories.json's scope note, no existing helper covers 'create a group, invite nobody' — every current helper pairs creation with an invite). Confirm no non-relay-bucket spec is touched, and if a helper file (e.g. `app/tests/e2e/helpers/group-setup.ts`) is extended, confirm the addition is a new exported helper function (additive) rather than a modification to any existing helper's behavior that other specs already depend on.

Per exploration.json's `single_user` note, this spec needs NO second `browser.newContext()` — a rare single-user groups spec — yet still requires the relay/Docker bucket because group creation alone publishes a KeyPackage. Confirm the spec uses exactly one browser context/identity, and confirm it is registered in (not accidentally excluded from) the relay bucket's `testMatch` and NOT duplicated into the non-relay bucket.

Per exploration.json's `readiness_gating` note, arrival at the group list after abandon MUST be gated on a rendered readiness marker (`groups-empty-state` or `groups-list`, mirroring `groups-lifecycle.spec.ts`'s `bootUser` pattern at lines 9-29), NEVER a fixed `waitForTimeout` used to assert the outcome under test. Confirm the spec's post-abandon assertion polls/waits on `getByTestId('groups-empty-state').or(getByTestId('groups-list'))` (or an equivalent readiness-gated wait) rather than a bare timeout — though a fixed settle-wait remains acceptable for pre-abandon infra steps (e.g. the ~3s post-create MLS-init/KeyPackage-publish settle) that are not themselves the assertion under test.

Per exploration.json's `solo_group_gap` note, no existing e2e helper covers 'create a group, invite nobody.' Confirm the create-only flow is lifted from (or closely mirrors) `groups-lifecycle.spec.ts:47-59` or `groups-leave-intent.spec.ts:99-109`: `create-group-btn` → fill `create-group-name-input` → `create-group-submit-btn` → wait for group text + modal close + `dismissErrorOverlay` + settle for MLS init/KeyPackage publish — and confirm it stops short of any invite step (no `inviteContactViaPicker` or `createGroupAndInvite` call), since inviting even one contact would make `memberPubkeys.length` ≥ 2 and defeat the entire point of the scenario.

Per exploration.json's `no_collision` and `existing_leave_e2e` notes, confirm this new spec uses a `GROUP_NAME` constant distinct from every existing groups spec's constant (per the established one-constant-per-spec convention), and that `abandon-group-notice`/`abandon-group-confirm-btn` are queried by no other spec today (net-new testids with zero pre-existing references). A collision on either axis risks cross-spec state bleed on the shared relay, which is the project's chronic source of flaky, hard-to-diagnose failures.

AC-STRUCT-2 is the cross-cutting guarantee spanning S1-S4: AC-ELIG-* and AC-SELECT-* (S1) are satisfiable by pure functions nothing calls; the enforcement that the feature is actually REACHABLE end-to-end is THIS story's AC-FLOW-1, which must drive a real solo group through the real `LeaveGroupButton` component and the real `MarmotContext.getLiveMemberPubkeys` / `leaveGroupImpl` wiring — not a mocked or stubbed path. Confirm the spec exercises the actual production component tree (no test-only bypass of the modal, no direct call into `leaveGroupImpl` from the test process): the test clicks the real leave button, the real modal, the real confirm button, and observes the real navigation outcome, proving the live-state read, the decision function, and the modal render are all wired together — not merely each independently correct in isolation.

A user who creates a group and invites nobody MUST be able to open the leave modal, activate `abandon-group-confirm-btn`, and arrive at the group list with the abandoned group absent from it. The URL assertion MUST be anchored to `/^\/groups\/?$/` — NOT a bare `/\/groups/` substring match (the project has a documented gotcha for this exact mistake at `dm-feedback-channel.spec.ts:162-164` / `groups-invite-guidance-state.spec.ts:73-75`, and `story-01-scaffold.spec.ts:44` uses the looser, wrong form). Since `next.config.mjs:22` sets `trailingSlash: true`, `router.push('/groups')` (`LeaveGroupButton.tsx:63`) resolves to `/groups/`, and an unanchored match would ALSO accept `/groups?id=…` — i.e. a test that never actually navigated away could still pass. Provide the exact `toHaveURL` assertion used and confirm it is the anchored regex form; confirm arrival is gated on a rendered readiness marker per VQ-S4-003; and confirm the abandoned group's name is asserted absent from the rendered list (not merely that some navigation occurred).

After an abandon completes, RELOADING the group list MUST NOT show the abandoned group — this discriminates a true purge from a mere render-state removal (e.g. a component that filters the group out of its in-memory list without actually deleting the underlying storage record). Confirm the spec performs an explicit page reload (or equivalent full remount) AFTER the initial post-abandon assertion, re-waits for the readiness marker, and asserts the group is STILL absent. A spec that only checks the pre-reload state would pass on a broken implementation that merely hides the group without purging it — so this assertion is only meaningful if it is a genuinely separate step from AC-FLOW-1's, not folded into the same un-reloaded check.

The spec file MUST be named `groups-abandon-*.spec.ts` so `playwright.config.ts:36-41`'s `testMatch: ['groups-*.spec.ts', 'dm-*.spec.ts']` (gated by `E2E_GROUPS`) routes it into the relay bucket — a name outside that glob would land it in the non-relay bucket, where it would fail for want of Docker/strfry infrastructure. Confirm the actual filename is exactly `app/tests/e2e/groups-abandon-last-member.spec.ts` (or another name matching `groups-abandon-*.spec.ts`), and run `E2E_GROUPS=1 node scripts/run-e2e.mjs tests/e2e/groups-abandon-last-member.spec.ts --list` (mirroring the `run_one_spec` recipe in exploration.json) to confirm the runner picks it up under the relay bucket's test-match glob, not the non-relay bucket's test-ignore.
